mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs due to recession fears.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth that sustains corporate earnings expectations, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce a statistically significant positive mean 60-day return (p > 0.05) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, resulting in positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns during periods where FEDFUNDS increases by more than 25 basis points per quarter.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 5.0% creates a high discount rate environment that compresses equity multiples, triggering immediate negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 65% of trading days following a FEDFUNDS reading above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, supporting positive quarterly equity performance.
failure_condition: The joint condition of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline produces a negative mean 60-day return or a Rank IC below 0.01 over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect increased construction activity and consumer confidence in durable goods, which translates into positive corporate revenue expectations and monthly equity gains.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 20-day forward returns, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.3 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production during a demand shock regime signals immediate supply chain bottlenecks and earnings downgrades, causing sharp negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 58% of cases, invalidating the supply contraction signal.
